Military unit

The **76th Regiment of Foot** was a [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army") regiment, raised in 1787. Under the [Childers Reforms](/wiki/Childers_Reforms "Childers Reforms") it amalgamated with the [33rd (Duke of Wellington's) Regiment](/wiki/Duke_of_Wellington%27s_Regiment "Duke of Wellington's Regiment") to form the [Duke of Wellington's Regiment](/wiki/Duke_of_Wellington%27s_Regiment "Duke of Wellington's Regiment") in 1881.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=76th_Regiment_of_Foot&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2c/Sir_Thomas_Musgrave%2C_7th_Bt_by_Georg_Siegmund_Facius%2C_after_Lemuel_Francis_Abbott_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Sir_Thomas_Musgrave%2C_7th_Bt_by_Georg_Siegmund_Facius%2C_after_Lemuel_Francis_Abbott_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Sir_Thomas_Musgrave,_7th_Bt_by_Georg_Siegmund_Facius,_after_Lemuel_Francis_Abbott_(cropped).jpg)

[Sir Thomas Musgrave, 7th Baronet](/wiki/Sir_Thomas_Musgrave,_7th_Baronet "Sir Thomas Musgrave, 7th Baronet"), founder of the regiment, by Georg Siegmund Facius after [Lemuel Francis Abbott](/wiki/Lemuel_Francis_Abbott "Lemuel Francis Abbott").

### Formation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=76th_Regiment_of_Foot&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Formation")]

The regiment was raised by [Sir Thomas Musgrave, 7th Baronet](/wiki/Sir_Thomas_Musgrave,_7th_Baronet "Sir Thomas Musgrave, 7th Baronet") for service in [India](/wiki/India "India") as the **76th Regiment of Foot** in October 1787.[[1]](#cite_note-regts-1) In accordance with the Declaratory Act 1788 the cost of raising the regiment was recharged to [East India Company](/wiki/East_India_Company "East India Company") on the basis that the act required that expenses "should be defrayed out of the revenues" arising there.[[1]](#cite_note-regts-1) The majority of recruits were raised from [Nottingham](/wiki/Nottingham "Nottingham") and [Leicestershire](/wiki/Leicestershire "Leicestershire"), but many of them also came from the Musgrave family estates around Hayton Castle, near [Aspatria](/wiki/Aspatria "Aspatria"), [Cumbria](/wiki/Cumbria "Cumbria").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The Royal Warrant for their raising was issued on 12 October 1787 and read:

**GEORGE R.**  
*Whereas We have thought fit to order a Regt of Foot to be*  
*forthwith raised under your Command, which is to consist of ten*  
*Companies, with 3 Sergts, 4 Corpls, 2 Drumrs & 71 private Men*  
*in each, with two Fifers to the Grenadier Compy and one*  
*Compy, of 8 Sergts, 8 Corpls, 4 Drumrs & 30 private Men with*  
*the usuals Comd. Officers, these are to authorise you by Beat of*  
*Drum or otherwise to raise so many Men in any Country or part*  
*of our Kingdom of Great Britain as shall be wanted to complete*  
*the said Regt, to the above mentioned numbers. And all above*  
*Given the 12th October. 1787 in the 27th Year of Our Reign.*  
*By H.M.'s Command (Sd.) Geo. Yonge*[[3]](#cite_note-3)

### India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=76th_Regiment_of_Foot&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: India")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Aligarh_fort%2C_Aligarh.jpg/250px-Aligarh_fort%2C_Aligarh.jpg)](/wiki/File:Aligarh_fort,_Aligarh.jpg)

[Moat](/wiki/Moat "Moat") at [Aligarh Fort](/wiki/Aligarh_Fort "Aligarh Fort") where the siege took place in September 1803

The regiment embarked for India in 1788 for service in the [Third Anglo-Mysore War](/wiki/Third_Anglo-Mysore_War "Third Anglo-Mysore War") and saw action at the [Siege of Bangalore](/wiki/Siege_of_Bangalore "Siege of Bangalore") in February 1791[[4]](#cite_note-4) and the [Siege of Seringapatam](/wiki/Siege_of_Seringapatam_(1792) "Siege of Seringapatam (1792)") in February 1792.[[5]](#cite_note-locations-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) The regiment also saw service in the [Second Anglo-Maratha War](/wiki/Second_Anglo-Maratha_War "Second Anglo-Maratha War") and fought at the [Siege of Aligarh](/wiki/Siege_of_Aligarh "Siege of Aligarh") in September 1803.[[7]](#cite_note-Thackeray-7) The regiment laid siege to [Aligarh Fort](/wiki/Aligarh_Fort "Aligarh Fort"), a fort commanded by a French mercenary in Maratha service [Pierre Cuillier-Perron](/wiki/Pierre_Cuillier-Perron "Pierre Cuillier-Perron"), and captured it.[[7]](#cite_note-Thackeray-7) The Marathas prepared for the siege by lining 14 ditches around the fort with sword-blades and poisoned [chevaux-de-frise](/wiki/Cheval_de_frise "Cheval de frise").[[7]](#cite_note-Thackeray-7) The walls were reinforced with Maratha artillery, and the defenders also used tigers and lions of [Scindia](/wiki/Scindia "Scindia")'s menagerie.[[7]](#cite_note-Thackeray-7) The British suffered 900 casualties in capturing the fort.[[7]](#cite_note-Thackeray-7) The regiment went on to fight at the [Battle of Delhi](/wiki/Battle_of_Delhi_(1803) "Battle of Delhi (1803)") in September 1803, the [Battle of Laswari](/wiki/Battle_of_Laswari "Battle of Laswari") in November 1803, and the [Battle of Deeg](/wiki/Battle_of_Deeg "Battle of Deeg") in November 1804.[[5]](#cite_note-locations-5) For their distinguished service in these actions, [King George III](/wiki/George_III_of_the_United_Kingdom "George III of the United Kingdom") authorized the regiment to have the word "[Hindoostan](/wiki/Hindustan "Hindustan")" emblazoned upon the [regimental colours](/wiki/Colours,_standards_and_guidons "Colours, standards and guidons"), along with an elephant badge with a [howdah](/wiki/Howdah "Howdah") atop the elephant, also inscribed with the word "Hindoostan".[[8]](#cite_note-8) The regiment returned to England and became the **76th (Hindoostan) Regiment of Foot** in October 1806.[[1]](#cite_note-regts-1)

### Napoleonic Wars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=76th_Regiment_of_Foot&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Napoleonic Wars")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e9/Battle_of_Plattsburgh_II.jpg/250px-Battle_of_Plattsburgh_II.jpg)](/wiki/File:Battle_of_Plattsburgh_II.jpg)

The [Battle of Plattsburgh](/wiki/Battle_of_Plattsburgh "Battle of Plattsburgh"), September 1814

In 1807, the regiment was deployed to [Jersey](/wiki/Jersey "Jersey") in the [Channel Islands](/wiki/Channel_Islands "Channel Islands") for garrison duty, remaining there until 1808, when it was deployed to Spain to take part in the [Peninsular War](/wiki/Peninsular_War "Peninsular War").[[5]](#cite_note-locations-5) The regiment took part in the [Battle of Corunna](/wiki/Battle_of_Corunna "Battle of Corunna") in January 1809 and was evacuated from the Peninsula later that month.[[5]](#cite_note-locations-5) The regiment took part in the disastrous [Walcheren Campaign](/wiki/Walcheren_Campaign "Walcheren Campaign") in autumn 1809 and, having reverted to the title of **76th Regiment of Foot** in 1812,[[1]](#cite_note-regts-1) returned to the Peninsula in 1813 seeing action at the [Battle of Nivelle](/wiki/Battle_of_Nivelle "Battle of Nivelle") in November 1813[[9]](#cite_note-9) and the [Battle of the Nive](/wiki/Battle_of_the_Nive "Battle of the Nive") in December 1813.[[10]](#cite_note-10) It then embarked for North America for service in the [War of 1812](/wiki/War_of_1812 "War of 1812") and saw action at the [Battle of Plattsburgh](/wiki/Battle_of_Plattsburgh "Battle of Plattsburgh") in September 1814.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### The Victorian era

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=76th_Regiment_of_Foot&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: The Victorian era")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2e/RebeccaRiots.gif/250px-RebeccaRiots.gif)](/wiki/File:RebeccaRiots.gif)

Depiction of the Rebecca Riots, *Illustrated London News* 1843

The regiment did not return from North America until 1827.[[5]](#cite_note-locations-5) It was garrisoned in [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland "Ireland") until 1834 when it departed for the [West Indies](/wiki/West_Indies "West Indies").[[12]](#cite_note-Hayden,_p._112-12) The regiment was posted to the [Garrison](/wiki/Bermuda_Garrison "Bermuda Garrison") of the [Imperial fortress](/wiki/Imperial_fortress "Imperial fortress") [colony](/wiki/British_Overseas_Territory "British Overseas Territory") of [Bermuda](/wiki/Bermuda "Bermuda"), part of [British North America](/wiki/British_North_America "British North America"), from 20 May 1840,[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15) until 9 November 1841,[[16]](#cite_note-16) and on to [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada") in 1841 before returning home in 1842.[[12]](#cite_note-Hayden,_p._112-12) The regiment were deployed to South Wales later in the year to help suppress the [Rebecca Riots](/wiki/Rebecca_Riots "Rebecca Riots").[[17]](#cite_note-capewell-17) After that the regiment went to [Corfu](/wiki/Corfu "Corfu") in 1848 and on to [Malta](/wiki/Malta "Malta") in 1850 before sailing for [Saint John, New Brunswick](/wiki/Saint_John,_New_Brunswick "Saint John, New Brunswick") in March 1853.[[18]](#cite_note-18) It was garrisoned at [Fredericton](/wiki/Fredericton "Fredericton") in [New Brunswick](/wiki/New_Brunswick "New Brunswick")[[17]](#cite_note-capewell-17) before embarking for home again in September 1857.[[19]](#cite_note-19) It embarked for India in September 1863[[20]](#cite_note-20) and was stationed in [Fort St. George, Madras](/wiki/Fort_St._George,_India "Fort St. George, India")[[17]](#cite_note-capewell-17) before moving on to [Burma](/wiki/Myanmar "Myanmar") in January 1868, returning to India again in 1870 and sailing for England in 1876.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

As part of the [Cardwell Reforms](/wiki/Cardwell_Reforms "Cardwell Reforms") of the 1870s, where single-battalion regiments were linked together to share a single depot and recruiting district in the United Kingdom, the 76th was linked with the [33rd (Duke of Wellington's) Regiment](/wiki/Duke_of_Wellington%27s_Regiment "Duke of Wellington's Regiment"), and assigned to district no. 9 at [Wellesley Barracks](/wiki/Wellesley_Barracks "Wellesley Barracks") in [Halifax](/wiki/Halifax,_West_Yorkshire "Halifax, West Yorkshire").[[22]](#cite_note-training-22) On 1 July 1881 the [Childers Reforms](/wiki/Childers_Reforms "Childers Reforms") came into effect and the regiment amalgamated with the 33rd (Duke of Wellington's) Regiment to form the [Duke of Wellington's Regiment](/wiki/Duke_of_Wellington%27s_Regiment "Duke of Wellington's Regiment").[[1]](#cite_note-regts-1)

## Battle honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=76th_Regiment_of_Foot&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Battle honours")]

The regiment's battle honours were:[[1]](#cite_note-regts-1)

* Hindoostan
* Peninsular War: [Nive](/wiki/Battle_of_Nive "Battle of Nive"), Peninsula

(410) men under the command of Colonel Clark, for Bermuda.-The Racehorse, will, we understand, return to Barbados. (The Vestal and Racehorse, were signalled to the southward last evening.)"
